Maxwell’s equations express, formally, properties electromagnetic waves.Gauss’s Law Electricity
\[ 
abla \cdot \mathbf{D} = ho_v \]Gauss’s Law Electricity

\[

abla\) = Vector differential operator.\(\mathbf{D}\) = Electric flux density.\(\mathbf{B}\) = Magnetic flux density.\(\mathbf{E}\) = Electric field intensity.\(\mathbf{H}\) = Magnetic field intensity.\(ho_v\) = Volume charge density.\(\mathbf{J}\) = Current density.\( rac{\partial \mathbf{D}}{\partial t}\) = Displacement current density.

heading: 2.1.2 Stoke’s Theorem Physics
text:
Stokes’ theorem relates circulation vector field \(\mathbf{}\) around closed path \(L\) surface integral curl open surface \(S\) bounded \(L\) [1]:\[ \int_S (\nabla \times \mathbf{}) \cdot d\mathbf{S} = \oint_L \mathbf{} \cdot d\mathbf{l}\]:
* \(d\mathbf{S}\) surface element vector [1].
* orientation follows right-hand rule [2].

heading: 2.1.3 Stoke’s Theorem for Flux Integral
text:
\[\iint_S (\nabla \times \mathbf{F}) \cdot \mathbf{n} \, dA = \oint_C \mathbf{F} \cdot \mathbf{r}'(s) \, ds\].,\[\iint_S (\text{curl} \times \mathbf{F}) \cdot \mathbf{n} \, dA = \oint_C \mathbf{F} \cdot \mathbf{r}'(s) \, ds\].formula:
* \(S\) piecewise smooth oriented surface space
* \(C\) piecewise smooth simple closed curve boundary \(S\)
* \(\mathbf{n}\) unit normal vector \(S\).
* \(\mathbf{r}'(s)\) unit tangent vector \(C\).
* \(s\) arc length \(C\).
* \(\mathbf{F}(x, y, z)\) continuous vector function

heading: 2.1.4 Surface Integrals and Fundamental Theorem of Calculus
text: \[\int_D d\omega = \int_{\partial D} \omega\]\(\omega\): differential \((k-1)\)-form.\(d\omega\): exterior derivative form \(\omega\).\(D\): \(k\)-dimensional domain integration.\(\partial D\): oriented boundary domain \(D\).

heading: 2.3 MATHEMARICS APPENDIX
text:
Stoke’s TheoremLet \(S\) piecewise smooth oriented surface space, let boundary \(S\) piecewise smooth simple closed curve \(C\). continuous first partial derivatives domain containing \(S\), :\[\iint_S (\nabla \times \mathbf{F}) \cdot \mathbf{n} \, dA = \oint_C \mathbf{F} \cdot \mathbf{r}'(s) \, ds\].,\[\iint_S (\text{curl} \times \mathbf{F}) \cdot \mathbf{n} \, dA = \oint_C \mathbf{F} \cdot \mathbf{r}'(s) \, ds\].formula:
* \(\mathbf{n}\) unit normal vector \(S\).
* \(\mathbf{r}'(s)\) unit tangent vector \(C\).
* \(s\) arc length \(C\).
* \(\mathbf{F}(x, y, z)\) continuous vector functionFluxTotal amount field fluid passing surface.Double IntegralThe double integral defined following identity:
\[\iint_R f(x, y) \, dA = \lim_{m, n \\infty} \sum_{=1}^m \sum_{j=1}^n f(x_{ij}^*, y_{ij}^*) \Delta \].
* \(R\) closed bounded region \(xy\)-plane function integrated [1, 2].
* \(m\) \(n\) number subrectangles region \(R\) partitioned [3, 4].
* \(R_{ij}\) smaller subrectangles resulting dividing \(R\) using grid lines parallel axes [4, 5].
* \(\Delta \) represents area subrectangle \(R_{ij}\) [4, 6].
* \((x_{ij}^*, y_{ij}^*)\) arbitrary sample point chosen within subrectangle \(R_{ij}\) [4, 6].
* limit taken \(m\) \(n\) approach infinity maximum diameter (greatest distance two points within subregion) approaches zero [3, 7].
* function \(f(x, y)\), integrand, typically assumed continuous \(R\) ensure limit exists independent choice sample points [7, 8].Double Integral Notational VariationsThere exist double integral two equivalent notations:
* Differential Area Form: \(\displaystyle \iint_R f(x, y) \, dA\).
* Coordinate Form: \(\displaystyle \iint_R f(x, y) \, dx \, dy\).Vector Differential OperatorThe vector differential operator expressing partial differentiation vector\[\nabla = \frac{\partial}{\partial x} \mathbf{} + \frac{\partial}{\partial y} \mathbf{j} + \frac{\partial}{\partial z} \mathbf{k}\]\(\nabla\) vector differential operator, commonly referred del nabla.\(\nabla\) vector differential operator, commonly referred del nabla.\(\dfrac{\partial}{\partial x}, \dfrac{\partial}{\partial y}, \text{ } \dfrac{\partial}{\partial z}\) partial derivatives.\(\dfrac{\partial}{\partial x}, \dfrac{\partial}{\partial y}, \text{ } \dfrac{\partial}{\partial z}\) partial derivatives.\(\mathbf{}, \mathbf{j}, \text{ } \mathbf{k}\) standard basis unit vectors pointing positive directions \(x, y, \text{ } z\) axes, respectively\(\mathbf{}, \mathbf{j}, \text{ } \mathbf{k}\) standard basis unit vectors pointing positive directions \(x, y, \text{ } z\) axes, respectivelyCurlLet \(\mathbf{v}(x, y, z)\) differentiable vector function expressed Cartesian coordinates components \([v_1, v_2, v_3]\), : \[\mathbf{v} = v_1 \mathbf{} + v_2 \mathbf{j} + v_3 \mathbf{k}\]
curl \(\mathbf{v}\) (also denoted \(\nabla \times \mathbf{v}\) \(\text{rot } \mathbf{v}\)) defined following symbolic determinant: \[\text{curl } \mathbf{v} = \nabla \times \mathbf{v} = \begin{vmatrix} \mathbf{} & \mathbf{j} & \mathbf{k} \\ \frac{\partial}{\partial x} & \frac{\partial}{\partial y} & \frac{\partial}{\partial z} \\ v_1 & v_2 & v_3 \end{vmatrix}\]DivergenceDifferentiation Vector\[\mathbf{v}'(t) = \lim_{\Delta t \0} \frac{\mathbf{v}(t + \Delta t) - \mathbf{v}(t)}{\Delta t}\].Vector Function\[\mathbf{r}(t) = \langle f(t), g(t), h(t) \rangle = f(t)\mathbf{} + g(t)\mathbf{j} + h(t)\mathbf{k}\]
- \(f, g, h\) components \(\mathbf{r}(t)\)

heading: 3.0.3 Double Integral
text:
double integral defined following identity:
\[\iint_R f(x, y) \, dA = \lim_{m, n \\infty} \sum_{=1}^m \sum_{j=1}^n f(x_{ij}^*, y_{ij}^*) \Delta \].
* \(R\) closed bounded region \(xy\)-plane function integrated [1, 2].
* \(m\) \(n\) number subrectangles region \(R\) partitioned [3, 4].
* \(R_{ij}\) smaller subrectangles resulting dividing \(R\) using grid lines parallel axes [4, 5].
* \(\Delta \) represents area subrectangle \(R_{ij}\) [4, 6].
* \((x_{ij}^*, y_{ij}^*)\) arbitrary sample point chosen within subrectangle \(R_{ij}\) [4, 6].
* limit taken \(m\) \(n\) approach infinity maximum diameter (greatest distance two points within subregion) approaches zero [3, 7].
* function \(f(x, y)\), integrand, typically assumed continuous \(R\) ensure limit exists independent choice sample points [7, 8].

heading: 3.0.4 Double Integral Notational Variations
text:
exist double integral two equivalent notations:
* Differential Area Form: \(\displaystyle \iint_R f(x, y) \, dA\).
* Coordinate Form: \(\displaystyle \iint_R f(x, y) \, dx \, dy\).

heading: 3.0.5 Projection
text: definition (vector projection) vector representing “shadow” vector \(\mathbf{b}\) cast onto line containing vector \(\mathbf{}\), denoted \(\text{proj}_{\mathbf{}} \mathbf{b}\). Geometrically, vector common initial point foot perpendicular dropped tip \(\mathbf{b}\) onto line containing \(\mathbf{}\). calculated using following formula:\(\text{proj}_{\mathbf{}} \mathbf{b} = \left( \dfrac{\mathbf{} \cdot \mathbf{b}}{|\mathbf{}|^2} \right) \mathbf{}\)\(\mathbf{}, \mathbf{b}\) vectors coordinate system.\(\mathbf{} \cdot \mathbf{b}\) dot product two vectors.\(|\mathbf{}|\) magnitude vector \(\mathbf{}\).definition (scalar projection) signed magnitude vector projection \(\mathbf{b}\) onto \(\mathbf{}\), also referred component \(\mathbf{b}\) along \(\mathbf{}\), denoted \(\text{comp}_{\mathbf{}} \mathbf{b}\). represents length “shadow” positive angle vectors acute negative obtuse. defined following formulas:\(\text{comp}_{\mathbf{}} \mathbf{b} = \dfrac{\mathbf{} \cdot \mathbf{b}}{|\mathbf{}|}\)\(\text{comp}_{\mathbf{}} \mathbf{b} = |\mathbf{b}| \cos \theta\)\(\mathbf{}, \mathbf{b}\) vectors coordinate system.\(\theta\) angle vectors \(\mathbf{}\) \(\mathbf{b}\).\(|\mathbf{b}|\) magnitude vector \(\mathbf{b}\).

heading: 5.0.1 Forms
text:
Forms functions take vectors input return scalars. 1-form takes single vector input, 2-form takes two vectors input, .definition (1-form = linear functional = covector = covariant vector) linear, real-valued function single vector input, \(\boldsymbol{\omega}: \mathbf{V} \rightarrow \mathbb{R}\), satisfies following conditions vectors scalars:(Additivity) \(\boldsymbol{\omega}(\mathbf{u} + \mathbf{v}) = \boldsymbol{\omega}(\mathbf{u}) + \boldsymbol{\omega}(\mathbf{v})\).(Homogeneity) \(\boldsymbol{\omega}(k\mathbf{v}) = k \cdot \boldsymbol{\omega}(\mathbf{v})\).
- \(\mathbf{V}\) real vector space.
- \(\mathbb{R}\) set real numbers.
- \(\boldsymbol{\omega}\) 1-form.
- \(\mathbf{u}, \mathbf{v}\) vectors \(\mathbf{V}\).
- \(k\) scalar.definition (2-form) bilinear, antisymmetric, real-valued function two vector inputs, \(\boldsymbol{\omega}: \mathbf{V} \times \mathbf{V} \rightarrow \mathbb{R}\), satisfies following conditions vectors scalars:(Bilinearity) \(\boldsymbol{\omega}(\mathbf{u} + b\mathbf{u}', \mathbf{v}) = \cdot \boldsymbol{\omega}(\mathbf{u}, \mathbf{v}) + b \cdot \boldsymbol{\omega}(\mathbf{u}', \mathbf{v})\) \(\boldsymbol{\omega}(\mathbf{u}, \mathbf{v} + b\mathbf{v}') = \cdot \boldsymbol{\omega}(\mathbf{u}, \mathbf{v}) + b \cdot \boldsymbol{\omega}(\mathbf{u}, \mathbf{v}')\).(Antisymmetry) \(\boldsymbol{\omega}(\mathbf{u}, \mathbf{v}) = -\boldsymbol{\omega}(\mathbf{v}, \mathbf{u})\).
- \(\mathbf{V}\) real vector space.
- \(\mathbb{R}\) set real numbers.
- \(\boldsymbol{\omega}\) 2-form.
- \(\mathbf{u}, \mathbf{u}', \mathbf{v}, \mathbf{v}'\) vectors \(\mathbf{V}\).
- \(, b\) scalars.definition (k-form) multilinear, completely antisymmetric, real-valued function \(k\) vector inputs, \(\boldsymbol{\omega}: \mathbf{V}^k \rightarrow \mathbb{R}\), satisfies following conditions vectors scalars:(k-Linearity) \(\boldsymbol{\omega}(\mathbf{v}_1, \dots, \mathbf{v}_i + b\mathbf{v}_i', \dots, \mathbf{v}_k) = \cdot \boldsymbol{\omega}(\mathbf{v}_1, \dots, \mathbf{v}_i, \dots, \mathbf{v}_k) + b \cdot \boldsymbol{\omega}(\mathbf{v}_1, \dots, \mathbf{v}_i', \dots, \mathbf{v}_k)\) argument \(\) \(1 \leq \leq k\).(Complete Antisymmetry) Swapping two vector inputs reverses sign output: \(\boldsymbol{\omega}(\dots, \mathbf{u}, \dots, \mathbf{v}, \dots) = -\boldsymbol{\omega}(\dots, \mathbf{v}, \dots, \mathbf{u}, \dots)\).
- \(\mathbf{V}\) real vector space.
- \(\mathbf{V}^k\) \(k\)-fold Cartesian product \(\mathbf{V}\).
- \(\mathbb{R}\) set real numbers.
- \(\boldsymbol{\omega}\) \(k\)-form.
- \(\mathbf{v}_1, \dots, \mathbf{v}_k, \mathbf{v}_i', \mathbf{u}, \mathbf{v}\) vectors \(\mathbf{V}\).
- \(k\) positive integer representing degree form.
- \(, b\) scalars.

- \(v_1, \dots, v_k, v_i' \V\) vectors.
- \(\\mathbb{R}\) scalar.definition (k-Tensor) multilinear function \(k\)-fold product vector space real numbers, \(T: V^k \rightarrow \mathbb{R}\), following condition applies:function \(T\) linear \(k\) arguments.
- \(V\) vector space.
- \(V^k\) \(k\)-fold Cartesian product \(V \times \dots \times V\).
- \(k\) positive integer representing degree tensor.
- \(\mathbb{R}\) set real numbers.
- \(\mathcal{L}^k(V)\) (\(\mathcal{J}^k(V)\)) vector space \(k\)-tensors \(V\).definition (Multilinear Function) function \(k\)-fold product vector space real numbers, \(T: V^k \rightarrow \mathbb{R}\), following conditions apply argument \(\) (\(1 \leq \leq k\)):\(T(v_1, \dots, v_i + v_i', \dots, v_k) = T(v_1, \dots, v_i, \dots, v_k) + T(v_1, \dots, v_i', \dots, v_k)\)\(T(v_1, \dots, av_i, \dots, v_k) = \cdot T(v_1, \dots, v_i, \dots, v_k)\)
- \(V\) vector space \(\mathbb{R}\).
- \(V^k\) \(k\)-fold Cartesian product \(V \times \dots \times V\).
- \(k\) positive integer representing number arguments, also called degree function.
- \(v_1, \dots, v_k, v_i' \V\) vectors.
- \(\\mathbb{R}\) scalar.definition (k-Fold Product) set constructed Cartesian product \(k\) copies single set, \(X^k\), following condition applies:resulting set consists ordered \(k\)-tuples coordinate element original set.
- \(k\) positive integer representing number repetitions.
- \(X\) underlying set, topological space, vector space.
- \(X^k\) (\(V^k\)) denotes \(k\)-fold Cartesian product \(X \times \dots \times X\).definition (Cartesian Product) set consisting possible ordered combinations elements collection sets, denoted \(X \times Y\) two sets, \(X_1 \times \dots \times X_n\) \(n\) sets, \(\prod_{\alpha \} X_\alpha\) indexed family, following conditions apply:two sets, product contains ordered pairs \((x, y)\) first component first set second component second.finite collection, product consists ordered \(n\)-tuples \((x_1, \dots, x_n)\) \(x_i\) element corresponding factor \(X_i\).arbitrary indexed family, product set functions \(x\) index set \(\) union sets \(x(\alpha) \X_\alpha\) \(\alpha \\).
- \(X, Y, X_i, X_\alpha\) non-empty sets

heading: 5.0.4 Functions
text:
definition (Alternating Function) property \(k\)-linear function \(k\)-fold product vector space real numbers, \(T: V^k \rightarrow \mathbb{R}\), following conditions apply:\(T(v_{\sigma(1)}, \dots, v_{\sigma(k)}) = (\text{sgn } \sigma) T(v_1, \dots, v_k)\) every permutation \(\sigma \S_k\).\(T(v_1, \dots, v_i, \dots, v_j, \dots, v_k) = -T(v_1, \dots, v_j, \dots, v_i, \dots, v_k)\) interchange two arguments.\(T(v_1, \dots, v_k) = 0\) whenever two vectors \(v_1, \dots, v_k\) equal.
- \(V\) vector space.
- \(V^k\) \(k\)-fold Cartesian product \(V \times \dots \times V\).
- \(S_k\) permutation group \(k\) objects.
- \(\text{sgn } \sigma\) sign permutation \(\sigma\), \(+1\) permutation even \(-1\) odd.
- \(v_1, \dots, v_k\) vectors \(V\).
- \(k\) positive integer representing degree function.definition (Alternating k-linear function) multilinear function \(k\)-fold product vector space real numbers, \(f: V^k \rightarrow \mathbb{R}\), following conditions apply:\(f(v_{\sigma(1)}, \dots, v_{\sigma(k)}) = (\text{sgn } \sigma) f(v_1, \dots, v_k)\) \(\sigma \S_k\).\(f(v_1, \dots, v_i, \dots, v_j, \dots, v_k) = -f(v_1, \dots, v_j, \dots, v_i, \dots, v_k)\) interchange two arguments.\(f(v_1, \dots, v_k) = 0\) whenever two vectors \(v_1, \dots, v_k\) equal.
- \(V\) vector space.
- \(V^k\) \(k\)-fold Cartesian product \(V \times \dots \times V\).
- \(k\) positive integer representing degree function.
- \(S_k\) permutation group \(k\) objects.
- \(\text{sgn } \sigma\) sign permutation \(\sigma\).
- \(v_1, \dots, v_k \V\) vectors.
- \(^k(V)\) (\(\Lambda^k(V^*)\)) vector space alternating \(k\)-linear functions \(V\).k-covector, multicovector degree k, alternating k-tensor synonyms alternating \(k\)-linear function.

heading: 5.0.5 Differential Form
text:
definition (Differential k-Form) function assigning alternating k-linear function point manifold \(M\), following condition applies:point \(p \M\), \(\omega(p)\) \(k\)-covector tangent space \(T_pM\).
- \(M\) smooth manifold.
- \(k\) non-negative integer representing degree form.
- \(T_pM\) tangent space \(M\) \(p\).
- \(T^*_p M\) (\(T^*_p(M)\)) cotangent space \(p\), defined dual space tangent space \(T_pM\).
- \(\Lambda^k(T^*_p M)\) (\(^k(T_pM)\)) vector space alternating \(k\)-tensors (also called \(k\)-covectors multicovectors) \(T_pM\).
- \(\omega\) smooth section vector bundle \(\Lambda^k(T^*M)\), \(k\)-th exterior power cotangent bundle.

heading: 5.0.6 Appendix
text:
definition (Linear Function) function vector space, \(f: \mathbf{V} \rightarrow \mathbb{R}\), satisfies following conditions vectors scalars:(Additivity) \(f(\mathbf{u} + \mathbf{v}) = f(\mathbf{u}) + f(\mathbf{v})\) .(Homogeneity) \(f(k\mathbf{v}) = k \cdot f(\mathbf{v})\).
- \(\mathbf{V}\) real vector space.
- \(\mathbf{u}, \mathbf{v} \\mathbf{V}\).
- \(k\) scalar.
